steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The skill is central ideas and details. The decisive clue is the passage's final sentence: understanding how to prevent horizontal gene transfer "might result in the mitigation of dangerous pathogens" — this frames the whole passage around practical benefit.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "A common perception of horizontal gene transfer is inaccurate.": The passage never mentions a common perception or corrects a misconception; it simply explains what HGT is and why studying it matters. ✗
  • "The results of new research into horizontal gene transfer cast doubt on earlier findings.": The passage makes no mention of earlier findings or contradictory research; no comparison of studies appears anywhere in the text. ✗
  • "The study of horizontal gene transfer may yield findings with useful applications.": Matches the passage exactly — HGT is explained, its role in antibiotic resistance is noted, and the final sentence ties understanding HGT to mitigating dangerous pathogens (a useful application). ✓
  • "Horizontal gene transfer is more common than was initially thought.": The passage states HGT is "common among prokaryotes" but never claims this contradicts a prior belief about its frequency. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
